back_translation: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to His disciples,
saying, "The scribes and the Pharisees sit on the seat of Moses.
Therefore everything that they tell you, practice and observe; but do not act according to their actions, for they speak but do not do.
They tie up heavy burdens and place them on the shoulders of people, but they themselves are not willing to move them even with the tip of a finger.
They do everything in order that people may see; for they enlarge their Scripture-cases and lengthen the tassels on the hem of their garments.
They love the chief seat at banquets and the chief seats in the synagogues,
and like to have people greet them respectfully in the marketplaces, and like to have people call them 'Rabbi.'
But as for you, do not let anyone call you 'Rabbi,' for your teacher is only one, and all of you are siblings to one another.
And do not call anyone in this world your father, for your Father is only One — the Father who dwells in heaven.
Do not let anyone call you 'leading-instructor,' for your leading-instructor is only one, namely the Christ.
The greatest among you must be the servant of you all.
Whoever exalts himself, that one will be brought low; and whoever humbles himself, that one will be lifted up.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you shut the kingdom of heaven in front of people. You yourselves do not enter, and you also do not allow those who are entering to go in.

Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel across sea and land in order to gain a single Jewish convert, and when he has come in, you make him a son of hell twice as much as you yourselves are.
Woe to you, blind guides! You say, 'Whoever swears by invoking the temple has no obligation, but whoever swears by invoking the gold of the temple — that one is bound by the oath.'
You foolish and blind ones! Between the gold and the temple that makes the gold holy, which is greater?
And you also say, 'Whoever swears by invoking the altar has no obligation, but whoever swears by invoking the offering that is on that altar — that one is bound by the oath.'
You blind ones! Between the offering and the altar that makes the offering holy, which is greater?
Therefore, the one who swears by invoking the altar swears by invoking the altar and also everything that is on that altar.
And the one who swears by invoking the temple swears by invoking the temple and also the One who divinely dwells in that temple.
And the one who swears by invoking the sky-heaven swears by invoking the throne of God and also the One who is enthroned upon that throne.

Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint, dill, and cumin, but you have neglected what is more important in the Law — namely justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These you ought to have done, and those other things should not have been neglected.
You blind guides! You strain out a gnat, but swallow a whole camel.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you cleanse the outside of the cup and the dish, but inside they are full of greed and intemperance.
You blind Pharisees, first cleanse the inside of the cup and dish, so that the outside also will be clean.

Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like burial-tombs that have been coated with white plaster, which on the outside appear beautiful, but on the inside are full of bones of dead people and every kind of uncleanness.
Likewise, you also: on the outside you appear to people to be righteous, but on the inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build burial-tombs for the prophets and decorate the memorials of the righteous,
and you say, 'If we had been in the days of our forefathers, we would not have joined them in shedding the blood of the prophets.'
Therefore you bear witness against yourselves that you are sons of the ones who killed the prophets.
As for you yourselves, fill up the measure of your forefathers.
You snakes! You offspring of vipers! How will you flee fully from the judgment in hell?

Because of this — behold — I am sending to you prophets, wise persons, and Scripture-teachers; some of them you will kill and crucify, and some of them you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from one city to another city,

so that the blood of all the righteous that has been shed upon the earth will fall upon you — from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Berechiah, the one whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
I tell you the truth: all of these things will fall upon the people of this generation.

O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who killed the prophets and stoned those whom He sent to you — how often I have desired to gather your children together, just as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you all were not willing.
Behold, the divine-house of you all will be left desolate.
For I tell you, you will not see Me from now on, until you say, 'May the One who comes in the name of the Lord receive blessing.'
